core_model.structure_summary: A first-order Ito equation with generic drift and time-dependent state-independent noise amplitude, paired with its time-reversed equation whose drift is corrected by the gradient of the log of the evolving density, and with a noise-free first-order flow that transports the same family of densities; the results bound the discrepancy between the distributions induced by the noisy and noise-free versions via the residual of the associated second-order density-evolution equation.
core_model.canonical_form:
dx_t = f(x_t,t)\,dt + g(t)\,dW_t;\quad dx_t =  ig(f(x_t,t) - g^2(t)\,
abla \log p(x_t,t) ig)dt + g(t)\,d ar{W}_t;\quad  rac{dx_t}{dt} = f(x_t,t) - 	frac{1}{2} g^2(t)\,
abla \log p(x_t,t);\quad \partial_t p + 
abla\cdot(f p) - 	frac{1}{2} g^2 
abla^2 p = 0

variables.meaning: vector-valued trajectory whose marginal density is the object of interest
continuous independent variable, run forward and reversed
standard Wiener increment with time-dependent state-independent amplitude
learned approximation to the gradient of the log density, whose error drives the studied gap
quadratic discrepancy between the true log-density gradient and its approximation, plus a residual of the density-evolution equation used as regularizer

deterministic_skeleton.summary: A first-order deterministic flow whose velocity field is a base drift minus half the squared noise amplitude times the gradient of the log of the density it transports, equivalently a continuity equation advecting that density.

notes: Three tightly linked faces of one structure appear: the forward/reverse noisy equations, the noise-free density-transport flow, and the second-order density-evolution equation connecting them. The core model is taken as this SDE/ODE/density-equation triple since the paper's main theorem bounds the distance between the noisy- and noise-free-induced distributions by the density-equation residual. Objective functionals (score matching, KL/Wasserstein bounds, residual regularizer) are analysis tools around this core.
